text: Last class we met the team and said what everyone is working on right now.
Today we write professional emails that get a clear and fast answer.
Recipient
Subject line
Attachment
Draft
Forward
Reply
Regarding
Acknowledge
Confirm
Clarify
Follow up
Request
I was wondering if you could send the report.
Would it be possible to confirm the date?
I would appreciate it if you could reply soon.
Could you please clarify the terms?
Hi Walyson, I am calling to follow up on your email regarding the shipment. Thank you for the draft.
You are welcome. I was wondering if you could confirm that the attachment opened correctly.
Yes, it opened fine. Could you clarify one point in the subject line about the delivery date?
Of course. Would it be possible to reply to my email with your questions, so I have them in writing?
Sure. I will reply this afternoon and forward it to my finance team.
Perfect. I would appreciate it if you could acknowledge the final version once you receive it.
I am writing regarding our order for next month.
I was wondering if you could send me the price list.
Please find the contract in the attachment.
Could you please confirm the delivery date?
I would appreciate it if you could reply by Friday.

Hi, this is Walyson. I am recording a quick note about an email I am sending to our supplier in Colombia. The subject line is short and clear: Request for an updated price list. I open the email politely, so I write: I am writing regarding our order for next month. Then I make my request in a soft way. I write: I was wondering if you could send me the updated price list and confirm the delivery date. I also add one document, so I write: please find the contract in the attachment. Before I send it, I read my draft one more time and I check the recipient at the top. At the end, I ask them to acknowledge the email and to reply by Friday. I always close with a polite line: I would appreciate a quick answer. Best regards, Walyson.

Hi Walyson, this is Marina in Geneva. I am calling to follow up on your email regarding the new contract. First, thank you for your message and for the attachment. I can confirm that I received the draft, and everything looks clear. I have only one small question: could you clarify the payment terms in section three? I was also wondering if you could forward the email to our finance team, so they can reply directly. I would appreciate it if we could confirm everything by Friday. If anything is not clear, please reach out to me. I will reply to your email today with my comments. Thank you again, and talk soon.

First, write a short and clear subject line. Next, open the email politely and say what it is regarding. Then make your request in a soft way, using could you or I was wondering if. After that, mention the attachment and ask the recipient to reply. Finally, close with a polite line and your name.
